excerpt: Amnesty reports a very wide range of civilians still inside Grozny after the ultimatum period.
transform_note: Used as the low and high bracket for the civilian exposure denominator, but not as the best point estimate.
confidence_note: Good for uncertainty bracketing, not for a precise point stock.

excerpt: Amnesty also records a narrower official band for civilians remaining in Grozny on 14 December.
transform_note: Retained as corroboration that the civilian stock was well below the prewar city population by the time of the assault.
confidence_note: Official statement rather than an independent count.

excerpt: The parliamentary briefing gives a single battle-period estimate of 20,000 civilians remaining in Grozny.
transform_note: Used as the best-point civilian stock for the main episode denominator.
confidence_note: Useful as a central battle-period point estimate, but still a secondary summary.

excerpt: RAND distinguishes a city-proper Russian assault force of roughly four to five thousand from the much larger theater deployment to Chechnya.
transform_note: Used directly in the combatant exposure denominator.
confidence_note: Good for city geometry; less certain as a day-by-day average throughout the full 44-day episode.

excerpt: RAND reports a city-proper rebel force band of about two to two-and-a-half thousand fighters.
transform_note: Used directly in the combatant exposure denominator.
confidence_note: City-bound and useful, but still an estimate recorded from the Russian side.

excerpt: Human Rights Watch documents at least thirty-eight civilians summarily executed in fourteen separate incidents in the district.
transform_note: Used directly as the civilian direct-death numerator for the split-out execution episode.
confidence_note: Strong minimum event count with clear civilian status, but still a lower bound because HRW notes additional allegations under investigation.
